title: A Difficult Fight: Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda
subtitle: Health workers struggle to stop a deadly virus in a region torn by conflict and fear.

summary.en: An Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo is spreading quickly through a region affected by long-term violence. Health experts are finding it hard to control the virus due to community mistrust and difficult living conditions. Neighboring countries have responded by closing borders, though international health leaders warn that such actions may not be effective. There is currently no vaccine for this specific strain of the virus, but officials hope to develop one soon. International support is increasing as leaders call for peace to allow medical teams to do their work.
summary.zh: 剛果民主共和國爆發的伊波拉疫情正迅速蔓延至一個受長期暴力影響的地區。由於社區的不信任感與艱困的生活條件，衛生專家發現難以控制病毒。鄰國已採取關閉邊界的應對措施，但國際衛生領袖警告此類行動可能無效。目前針對此特定病毒株尚無疫苗，但官員希望能儘快研發出來。隨著各國領袖呼籲和平以利醫療團隊執行任務，國際支援正持續增加。

paragraphs.en: A serious Ebola outbreak is currently spreading through the eastern part of the Democratic Republic of Congo. According to NPR Health, the situation is becoming very difficult for health workers who are trying to stop the virus in a region that has suffered from years of conflict. The outbreak is centered in a town called Mongbwalu, where thousands of people live. Health officials believe the virus was spreading for weeks before it was officially identified in mid-May.
paragraphs.zh: 一場嚴重的伊波拉病毒疫情目前正在剛果民主共和國（Democratic Republic of Congo）東部蔓延。根據美國國家公共廣播電台（NPR）健康頻道的報導，對於那些試圖在飽受多年戰亂摧殘的地區遏止病毒的醫護人員來說，情況正變得極其艱難。疫情集中在一個名為蒙布瓦盧（Mongbwalu）的城鎮，當地居住著數千名民眾。衛生官員認為，該病毒在五月中旬被正式確認之前，可能已經傳播了數週之久。

paragraphs.en: According to reports from Deutsche Welle (DW) Top, the current outbreak is caused by a specific type of the virus known as the Bundibugyo strain. This is a dangerous situation because there is currently no vaccine or treatment available for this version of the disease. While official numbers suggest hundreds of cases and deaths, health experts believe the true number of people affected is likely much higher. The virus may have been moving through the community without being noticed for some time.
paragraphs.zh: 根據德國之聲（Deutsche Welle）的報導，目前的疫情是由一種被稱為邦迪布焦（Bundibugyo）病毒株的特定類型所引起。這是一個危險的情況，因為目前針對這種版本的疾病尚無疫苗或治療方法。雖然官方數據顯示有數百起病例與死亡人數，但衛生專家認為，受影響的實際人數可能遠高於此。病毒可能在未被察覺的情況下，已在社區中傳播了一段時間。

paragraphs.en: Efforts to stop the spread of the virus are facing major challenges. In Mongbwalu, many people do not trust the hospital staff. Some residents believe that the illness is not real or that medical workers are actually causing the sickness. NPR Health reports that angry crowds have attacked the local hospital and even burned down a tent used to keep sick patients separate from others. These actions make it very hard for doctors to track who has been in contact with the virus and to provide safe care.
paragraphs.zh: 遏止病毒擴散的努力正面臨重大挑戰。在蒙布瓦盧，許多民眾並不信任醫院工作人員。部分居民認為這種疾病並非真實存在，或是醫護人員才是導致生病的元兇。美國國家公共廣播電台健康頻道報導指出，憤怒的群眾曾攻擊當地醫院，甚至燒毀了用於隔離病患的帳篷。這些行為使得醫生難以追蹤曾與病毒接觸的人員，也難以提供安全的照護。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the lack of trust, the region is also struggling with the effects of long-term violence. Many people live in crowded camps after being forced to leave their homes because of fighting between armed groups. Health workers must often travel through dangerous areas and cross checkpoints to reach those in need. The head of the World Health Organization recently described the situation as a "catastrophic collision of disease and conflict," noting that the virus is spreading faster than the medical response can keep up.
paragraphs.zh: 除了缺乏信任之外，該地區還深受長期暴力衝突的影響。許多人在被迫逃離家園後，只能居住在擁擠的營地中。醫護人員往往必須穿越危險區域並通過檢查哨，才能接觸到有需要的民眾。世界衛生組織（World Health Organization）負責人近期將此情況描述為「疾病與衝突的災難性碰撞」，並指出病毒擴散的速度已超過醫療應對的速度。

paragraphs.en: In response to the crisis, some neighboring countries have closed their borders to prevent the virus from entering their territory. However, international health leaders have expressed concern about these travel bans. According to Deutsche Welle (DW) Top, the World Health Organization does not support these restrictions, arguing that they do not help stop the disease and may even make the situation worse by slowing down aid. Meanwhile, other nations have taken different steps; STAT News reports that the United States has decided it will not bring American citizens who catch Ebola back to the country for treatment, choosing instead to send them to facilities in Europe.
paragraphs.zh: 為了應對這場危機，一些鄰國已經關閉邊界，以防止病毒進入其領土。然而，國際衛生領袖對這些旅遊禁令表示擔憂。根據德國之聲報導，世界衛生組織並不贊同這些限制措施，認為它們無助於遏止疾病，甚至可能因拖延援助而使情況惡化。與此同時，其他國家則採取了不同措施；根據 STAT 新聞報導，美國已決定不會將感染伊波拉的美國公民接回國內治療，而是選擇將他們送往歐洲的醫療機構。

paragraphs.en: Despite these many obstacles, there is hope for the future. The head of the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention stated that they are working hard to create a vaccine and medicine for this strain of Ebola. They believe that a vaccine could be ready by the end of 2026. Leaders are also calling for a ceasefire—an agreement to stop fighting—in the region to allow health teams to work safely and reach the people who need help most.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管面臨諸多障礙，未來仍有一線希望。非洲疾病管制暨預防中心（Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention）負責人表示，他們正致力於研發針對此種伊波拉病毒株的疫苗與藥物。他們相信疫苗有望在二〇二六年年底前準備就緒。各國領袖也呼籲該地區實施停火協議，以利醫療團隊能安全工作，並接觸到最需要幫助的民眾。

paragraphs.en: As the situation continues to develop, the focus remains on building trust with local communities and providing better support for the medical teams on the ground. The international community is watching closely, hoping that the combination of new medical tools and a more stable environment will eventually bring an end to this dangerous outbreak.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著情勢持續發展，重點仍放在建立與當地社區的信任，並為第一線醫療團隊提供更好的支援。國際社會正密切關注，期盼透過新醫療工具與更穩定環境的結合，最終能終結這場危險的疫情。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To move or progress at the same speed as something else.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「保持」，這裡指醫療應對速度趕不上病毒擴散速度。文中：noting that the virus is spreading faster than the medical response can keep up.

keyTerms.definitionEn: In the actual place where an event or activity is happening, rather than from a distance.
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面上是「在地上」，這裡指實際在災區工作的醫護人員。文中：providing better support for the medical teams on the ground.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To follow or monitor the progress or movement of someone or something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（軌道），這裡作動詞，指追蹤接觸者。文中：These actions make it very hard for doctors to track who has been in contact with the virus and to provide safe care.

sources.name: NPR Health
sources.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak is spiraling, with health workers struggling to contain the virus

sources.name: STAT News
sources.title: STAT+: As Ebola outbreak in Central Africa grows, the U.S. turns itself into a fortress

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: WHO chief arrives in Congo amid Ebola outbreak

quiz.question: What specific action did local residents in Mongbwalu take that hindered medical efforts?

options.A: They blocked the roads to prevent medical supplies from arriving.
options.B: They destroyed a facility used to isolate infected patients.
options.C: They refused to leave their homes to be vaccinated.
options.D: They protested at the border to demand better medicine.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，居民因不信任醫療人員，不僅攻擊醫院，還燒毀了用來隔離病患的帳篷（burned down a tent used to keep sick patients separate），這直接阻礙了醫療工作。選項 A 文章未提及封路；選項 C 文章提到的是對醫療的不信任，而非拒絕疫苗（目前也無疫苗）；選項 D 關於邊境的爭議是國際間的政策，與當地居民的抗議行為無關。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why is the current Ebola outbreak in the DRC particularly difficult to contain?

options.A: The virus is spreading primarily through international air travel.
options.B: Health workers are refusing to enter the region due to low pay.
options.C: The combination of social distrust and regional instability prevents effective aid.
options.D: The virus has mutated to become resistant to all known forms of sanitation.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第四段提到世界衛生組織負責人將此描述為「疾病與衝突的災難性碰撞」，結合第三段提到的居民不信任（social distrust）與第四段提到的長期暴力衝突（regional instability），顯示這些因素加總使得醫療救援難以推進。選項 A 文章未提及航空傳播；選項 B 文章提到的是危險與不信任，而非薪資問題；選項 D 文章僅提到無疫苗，並未提到病毒對衛生措施產生抗藥性。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the word 'collision' mean in the context of the phrase 'catastrophic collision of disease and conflict'?

options.A: A violent crash between two physical objects.
options.B: A meeting of two different things that causes a negative result.
options.C: A disagreement between two opposing political groups.
options.D: A sudden change in the direction of a medical study.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境下，「collision」並非指物理上的車禍或撞擊，而是隱喻兩種負面力量（疾病與衝突）同時發生並互相加劇，導致災難性後果。選項 A 是字面意義，不符合文意；選項 C 雖然提到衝突，但「collision」在此處指的是疾病與衝突這兩者的結合，而非僅指政治鬥爭；選項 D 完全偏離原意。

quiz.question: What is the primary focus of the article regarding the Ebola outbreak in the Congo?

options.A: To explain the biological history of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola.
options.B: To argue that international travel bans are the most effective way to stop the virus.
options.C: To criticize the United States for not bringing its citizens home.
options.D: To describe the complex challenges and ongoing efforts to manage the crisis.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。全文從疫情爆發、面臨的醫療與社會挑戰（不信任、衝突）、國際反應，到未來的疫苗研發希望，全面性地探討了這場危機的複雜性與應對措施。選項 A 僅是文章的一小部分細節；選項 B 與文章觀點相反，世衛組織並不贊同旅行禁令；選項 C 僅是關於美國政策的補充說明，並非文章主旨。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
